title: CANN 8.x requires NumPy <2.0 due to removed np.float_ alias
problem_description: CANN 8.1.RC1 tbe/te packages reference np.float_ which was deprecated in NumPy 1.x and removed entirely in NumPy 2.0+. When NumPy >=2.0 is installed in the venv (the default on Python 3.10+ via pip), importing tbe or te fails with AttributeError: 'numpy' has no attribute 'float_'. This blocks the entire NPU import preflight, causing the migration validation to fail before the server even launches.
rough_fix_approach: Pin numpy<2.0.0 in the project venv during Phase 2 (venv creation) BEFORE installing torch-npu or any CANN-dependent packages. The fix command is: pip install 'numpy<2.0.0'. This restores the np.float_ alias that CANN 8.1.RC1 expects. No code changes needed in the migrated project.
artifact_evidence: raw/phase_fix_dependency_attempt0.json:lines 1-14 (dependency fix result: FULL_PASS after numpy downgrade)
validated/phase_2_venv_create_canonical.json (installed_packages list, no numpy pinning on initial install)

involved_code_files.role: CANN toolkit system packages — tbe and te modules reference np.float_

reasoning: This is a hard dependency constraint that affects EVERY project migrated to CANN 8.x. NumPy 2.x is now the default on pip for Python 3.10+ environments, so any migration that doesn't pin numpy will fail at the import preflight stage. This is fully deterministic — either numpy is <2.0.0 and tbe/te imports work, or it's >=2.0.0 and they fail. It can be enforced as a pre-install dependency version constraint in Phase 2 (venv_create). The confidence is 0.95 because the error traceback and root cause are unambiguous, the fix is trivially verifiable, and the pattern generalizes to all CANN 8.x environments (not just 8.1.RC1).
